#!/usr/bin/env bash
# version-bumper.sh — Semantic version bumper based on conventional commits
#
# Reads a VERSION file (or package.json), analyses conventional commit messages,
# determines the appropriate version bump (major/minor/patch), updates the
# version file, generates a changelog entry, and prints the new version.
#
# Usage:
#   version-bumper.sh [--version-file FILE] [--commit-log FILE]
#
# Options:
#   --version-file FILE   Path to VERSION file or package.json (default: VERSION)
#   --commit-log FILE     Path to file containing commit messages, one per line.
#                         If omitted, reads from git log since last tag.
#
# Exit codes:
#   0  Success
#   1  General error (missing file, bad version, etc.)

set -euo pipefail

# ── Helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

die() {
  echo "ERROR: $*" >&2
  exit 1
}

# Parse the current version from a file.
# Supports plain VERSION files (just "1.2.3") and package.json ("version": "1.2.3").
parse_version() {
  local file="$1"

  [[ -f "$file" ]] || die "Version file not found: $file"

  local content
  content=$(<"$file")

  if [[ "$file" == *.json ]]; then
    # Extract version from JSON — lightweight, no jq dependency
    local ver
    ver=$(echo "$content" | grep -oP '"version"\s*:\s*"\K[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' || true)
    [[ -n "$ver" ]] || die "Could not parse version from $file"
    echo "$ver"
  else
    # Plain text file — expect semver on first non-empty line
    local ver
    ver=$(echo "$content" | grep -oP '^[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+' | head -1 || true)
    [[ -n "$ver" ]] || die "Could not parse version from $file"
    echo "$ver"
  fi
}

# Read commit messages from a file or git log.
read_commits() {
  local commit_log="$1"

  if [[ -n "$commit_log" ]]; then
    [[ -f "$commit_log" ]] || die "Commit log file not found: $commit_log"
    cat "$commit_log"
  else
    # Fall back to git log since last tag
    local last_tag
    last_tag=$(git describe --tags --abbrev=0 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    if [[ -n "$last_tag" ]]; then
      git log "${last_tag}..HEAD" --pretty=format:"%s"
    else
      git log --pretty=format:"%s"
    fi
  fi
}

# Determine bump type from conventional commit messages.
# Returns: major, minor, or patch
determine_bump() {
  local commits="$1"
  local bump="patch"

  while IFS= read -r msg; do
    [[ -z "$msg" ]] && continue

    # Breaking change — indicated by "BREAKING CHANGE" anywhere or "!" after type
    if echo "$msg" | grep -qiP '(BREAKING CHANGE|^[a-z]+(\(.+\))?!:)'; then
      bump="major"
      break  # major is the highest — no need to keep looking
    fi

    # feat -> minor bump (only upgrade, never downgrade)
    if echo "$msg" | grep -qP '^feat(\(.+\))?:'; then
      bump="minor"
    fi

    # fix -> patch (already the default, but be explicit for clarity)
    # Other prefixes (chore, docs, ci, etc.) don't trigger a bump beyond patch
  done <<< "$commits"

  echo "$bump"
}

# Apply a bump to a semver string.  Returns the new version.
bump_version() {
  local version="$1"
  local bump="$2"

  local major minor patch
  IFS='.' read -r major minor patch <<< "$version"

  case "$bump" in
    major)
      major=$((major + 1))
      minor=0
      patch=0
      ;;
    minor)
      minor=$((minor + 1))
      patch=0
      ;;
    patch)
      patch=$((patch + 1))
      ;;
    *)
      die "Unknown bump type: $bump"
      ;;
  esac

  echo "${major}.${minor}.${patch}"
}

# Write the new version back to the file.
write_version() {
  local file="$1"
  local old_version="$2"
  local new_version="$3"

  if [[ "$file" == *.json ]]; then
    # Replace version in JSON
    sed -i "s/\"version\"\s*:\s*\"${old_version}\"/\"version\": \"${new_version}\"/" "$file"
  else
    # Replace version in plain text file
    sed -i "s/${old_version}/${new_version}/" "$file"
  fi
}

# Generate a changelog entry from commit messages.
generate_changelog() {
  local new_version="$1"
  local commits="$2"
  local date
  date=$(date +%Y-%m-%d)

  echo "## [$new_version] - $date"
  echo ""

  local has_breaking=false has_features=false has_fixes=false has_other=false
  local breaking_items="" feature_items="" fix_items="" other_items=""

  while IFS= read -r msg; do
    [[ -z "$msg" ]] && continue

    if echo "$msg" | grep -qiP '(BREAKING CHANGE|^[a-z]+(\(.+\))?!:)'; then
      has_breaking=true
      breaking_items+="- $msg"$'\n'
    elif echo "$msg" | grep -qP '^feat(\(.+\))?:'; then
      has_features=true
      feature_items+="- $msg"$'\n'
    elif echo "$msg" | grep -qP '^fix(\(.+\))?:'; then
      has_fixes=true
      fix_items+="- $msg"$'\n'
    else
      has_other=true
      other_items+="- $msg"$'\n'
    fi
  done <<< "$commits"

  if $has_breaking; then
    echo "### Breaking Changes"
    echo "$breaking_items"
  fi
  if $has_features; then
    echo "### Features"
    echo "$feature_items"
  fi
  if $has_fixes; then
    echo "### Bug Fixes"
    echo "$fix_items"
  fi
  if $has_other; then
    echo "### Other"
    echo "$other_items"
  fi
}

# ── Main ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

main() {
  local version_file="VERSION"
  local commit_log=""

  # Parse arguments
  while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
      --version-file)
        version_file="$2"
        shift 2
        ;;
      --commit-log)
        commit_log="$2"
        shift 2
        ;;
      -h|--help)
        head -18 "$0" | tail -16
        exit 0
        ;;
      *)
        die "Unknown option: $1"
        ;;
    esac
  done

  # 1. Parse current version
  local current_version
  current_version=$(parse_version "$version_file")
  echo "Current version: $current_version"

  # 2. Read commit messages
  local commits
  commits=$(read_commits "$commit_log")
  [[ -n "$commits" ]] || die "No commits found"

  # 3. Determine bump type
  local bump
  bump=$(determine_bump "$commits")
  echo "Bump type: $bump"

  # 4. Compute new version
  local new_version
  new_version=$(bump_version "$current_version" "$bump")
  echo "New version: $new_version"

  # 5. Update version file
  write_version "$version_file" "$current_version" "$new_version"

  # 6. Generate changelog
  local changelog
  changelog=$(generate_changelog "$new_version" "$commits")
  echo ""
  echo "$changelog"

  # Append to CHANGELOG.md if it exists (or create it)
  if [[ -f CHANGELOG.md ]]; then
    local tmp
    tmp=$(mktemp)
    echo "$changelog" > "$tmp"
    echo "" >> "$tmp"
    cat CHANGELOG.md >> "$tmp"
    mv "$tmp" CHANGELOG.md
  else
    echo "$changelog" > CHANGELOG.md
  fi

  echo ""
  echo "VERSION_BUMPED=$new_version"
}

# Only run main when executed directly (not when sourced for testing)
if [[ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" == "${0}" ]]; then
  main "$@"
fi

name: Semantic Version Bumper

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, master]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main, master]
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      version_file:
        description: "Path to version file"
        required: false
        default: "VERSION"
      commit_log:
        description: "Path to commit log fixture"
        required: false
        default: ""

permissions:
  contents: read

jobs:
  bump-version:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Validate script syntax
        run: |
          bash -n version-bumper.sh
          echo "Syntax check passed"

      - name: Run version bumper
        id: bump
        run: |
          chmod +x version-bumper.sh

          # Determine version file
          VERSION_FILE="${{ github.event.inputs.version_file || 'VERSION' }}"
          COMMIT_LOG="${{ github.event.inputs.commit_log || '' }}"

          # Build args
          ARGS=(--version-file "$VERSION_FILE")
          if [ -n "$COMMIT_LOG" ]; then
            ARGS+=(--commit-log "$COMMIT_LOG")
          fi

          # Run the bumper and capture output
          OUTPUT=$(./version-bumper.sh "${ARGS[@]}" 2>&1) || {
            echo "$OUTPUT"
            echo "Version bumper failed"
            exit 1
          }
          echo "$OUTPUT"

          # Extract the new version from output
          NEW_VERSION=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -oP 'VERSION_BUMPED=\K[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+')
          echo "new_version=$NEW_VERSION" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "RESULT: version=$NEW_VERSION"

      - name: Show result
        run: |
          echo "Bumped to version: ${{ steps.bump.outputs.new_version }}"
          echo "Updated VERSION file:"
          cat VERSION 2>/dev/null || cat package.json 2>/dev/null || echo "No version file found"
          echo ""
          echo "Generated CHANGELOG.md:"
          cat CHANGELOG.md 2>/dev/null || echo "No changelog generated"

  test-cases:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    strategy:
      matrix:
        test:
          - name: patch-bump
            version_file: test/fixtures/version-1.0.0.txt
            commit_log: test/fixtures/commits-patch.txt
            expected_version: "1.0.1"
            expected_bump: patch
          - name: minor-bump
            version_file: test/fixtures/version-1.1.0.txt
            commit_log: test/fixtures/commits-minor.txt
            expected_version: "1.2.0"
            expected_bump: minor
          - name: major-bump
            version_file: test/fixtures/version-2.3.4.txt
            commit_log: test/fixtures/commits-major.txt
            expected_version: "3.0.0"
            expected_bump: major
          - name: breaking-footer
            version_file: test/fixtures/version-1.0.0.txt
            commit_log: test/fixtures/commits-breaking-footer.txt
            expected_version: "2.0.0"
            expected_bump: major
          - name: package-json
            version_file: test/fixtures/package.json
            commit_log: test/fixtures/commits-minor.txt
            expected_version: "3.2.0"
            expected_bump: minor
    steps:
      - name: Checkout repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Run test ${{ matrix.test.name }}
        shell: bash
        env:
          FIXTURE_VERSION_FILE: ${{ matrix.test.version_file }}
          FIXTURE_COMMIT_LOG: ${{ matrix.test.commit_log }}
          EXPECTED_VERSION: ${{ matrix.test.expected_version }}
          EXPECTED_BUMP: ${{ matrix.test.expected_bump }}
          TEST_NAME: ${{ matrix.test.name }}
        run: |
          chmod +x version-bumper.sh

          # Copy fixture version file so we don't modify the original
          if [[ "$FIXTURE_VERSION_FILE" == *.json ]]; then
            cp "$FIXTURE_VERSION_FILE" test_version_file.json
            VERSION_ARG="test_version_file.json"
          else
            cp "$FIXTURE_VERSION_FILE" test_version_file
            VERSION_ARG="test_version_file"
          fi

          OUTPUT=$(./version-bumper.sh \
            --version-file "$VERSION_ARG" \
            --commit-log "$FIXTURE_COMMIT_LOG" 2>&1)
          echo "$OUTPUT"

          # Assert exact version
          ACTUAL=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -oP 'VERSION_BUMPED=\K[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+')

          echo "TEST=$TEST_NAME"
          echo "EXPECTED_VERSION=$EXPECTED_VERSION"
          echo "ACTUAL_VERSION=$ACTUAL"

          if [ "$ACTUAL" = "$EXPECTED_VERSION" ]; then
            echo "PASS: Version matches ($ACTUAL)"
          else
            echo "FAIL: Expected $EXPECTED_VERSION but got $ACTUAL"
            exit 1
          fi

          # Assert bump type
          ACTUAL_BUMP=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -oP 'Bump type: \K\w+')
          echo "EXPECTED_BUMP=$EXPECTED_BUMP"
          echo "ACTUAL_BUMP=$ACTUAL_BUMP"

          if [ "$ACTUAL_BUMP" = "$EXPECTED_BUMP" ]; then
            echo "PASS: Bump type matches ($ACTUAL_BUMP)"
          else
            echo "FAIL: Expected bump $EXPECTED_BUMP but got $ACTUAL_BUMP"
            exit 1
          fi

content.text:
Good — the `catthehacker/ubuntu:act-latest` image is available. Now let me think about the bats test structure.

The matrix strategy will spawn multiple jobs. `act` with matrix jobs can be tricky. Let me think about how to structure this — I'll run act on specific jobs to keep things manageable.

For the test cases, I need to set up a temporary git repo each time (because act needs a git repo with the workflow). Let me create a helper that:
1. Creates a tmp dir
2. Initializes git
3. Copies all our files
4. Commits them
5. Runs `act push` on a specific job
6. Captures output

Let me also consider that matrix jobs with `act` need the `-j` flag to target specific jobs. But with matrix, act runs all matrix combinations for that job. I could instead create a simpler workflow approach — or just run the test-cases job and check all output at once.

#!/usr/bin/env bats
# test_workflow.bats — TDD tests for the semantic version bumper
#
# All functional tests run through the GitHub Actions workflow via `act`.
# Structural tests validate YAML and file references.
#
# Output from every act run is appended to act-result.txt.

# ── Setup / Teardown ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

ORIG_DIR=""
ACT_RESULT=""

setup_file() {
  ORIG_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$BATS_TEST_FILENAME")" && pwd)"
  ACT_RESULT="${ORIG_DIR}/act-result.txt"
  # Clear the results file at the start of the suite
  : > "$ACT_RESULT"
  export ORIG_DIR ACT_RESULT
}

setup() {
  ORIG_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$BATS_TEST_FILENAME")" && pwd)"
  ACT_RESULT="${ORIG_DIR}/act-result.txt"
}

# Helper: create a temporary git repo with all project files
create_test_repo() {
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)

  # Copy project files into the temp repo
  cp "${ORIG_DIR}/version-bumper.sh" "$tmpdir/"
  cp -r "${ORIG_DIR}/test" "$tmpdir/"
  mkdir -p "$tmpdir/.github/workflows"
  cp "${ORIG_DIR}/.github/workflows/semantic-version-bumper.yml" "$tmpdir/.github/workflows/"

  # Initialize git repo (act requires this)
  cd "$tmpdir"
  git init -b main
  git config user.email "test@test.com"
  git config user.name "Test"
  git add -A
  git commit -m "initial commit"

  echo "$tmpdir"
}

# Helper: run act and capture output, appending to act-result.txt
run_act() {
  local job="$1"
  shift
  local output
  local exit_code=0

  echo "===== ACT RUN: job=$job $(date -Iseconds) =====" >> "$ACT_RESULT"

  output=$(act push --rm \
    -j "$job" \
    -P ubuntu-latest=catthehacker/ubuntu:act-latest \
    --detect-event \
    "$@" 2>&1) || exit_code=$?

  echo "$output" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
  echo "===== EXIT CODE: $exit_code =====" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
  echo "" >> "$ACT_RESULT"

  # Make output available to the test
  echo "$output"
  return "$exit_code"
}

# RED: workflow YAML must exist
@test "workflow file exists" {

}

@test "workflow passes actionlint" {
  run actionlint "${ORIG_DIR}/.github/workflows/semantic-version-bumper.yml"
  echo "actionlint output: $output" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
  [ "$status" -eq 0 ]
}

# RED: workflow must have correct triggers
@test "workflow has push and pull_request triggers" {
  local wf="${ORIG_DIR}/.github/workflows/semantic-version-bumper.yml"
  grep -q "push:" "$wf"
  grep -q "pull_request:" "$wf"
  grep -q "workflow_dispatch:" "$wf"
}

# RED: workflow must define expected jobs
@test "workflow has bump-version and test-cases jobs" {
  local wf="${ORIG_DIR}/.github/workflows/semantic-version-bumper.yml"
  grep -q "bump-version:" "$wf"
  grep -q "test-cases:" "$wf"
}

# RED: workflow references version-bumper.sh which must exist
@test "workflow references existing script files" {
  local wf="${ORIG_DIR}/.github/workflows/semantic-version-bumper.yml"
  grep -q "version-bumper.sh" "$wf"
  [ -f "${ORIG_DIR}/version-bumper.sh" ]
  [ -x "${ORIG_DIR}/version-bumper.sh" ]
}

# RED: workflow must use actions/checkout@v4
@test "workflow uses actions/checkout@v4" {
  local wf="${ORIG_DIR}/.github/workflows/semantic-version-bumper.yml"
  grep -q "actions/checkout@v4" "$wf"
}

# RED: workflow must set permissions
@test "workflow sets permissions" {

  grep -q "permissions:" "$wf"
}

# RED: script passes shellcheck
@test "version-bumper.sh passes shellcheck" {

  [ "$status" -eq 0 ]
}

# RED: script passes bash -n syntax check
@test "version-bumper.sh passes bash syntax check" {
  run bash -n "${ORIG_DIR}/version-bumper.sh"
  [ "$status" -eq 0 ]
}

# ── Functional Tests via act ────────────────────────────────────────────────

# RED: bump-version job must succeed with default fixtures
@test "act: bump-version job succeeds" {
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir=$(create_test_repo)

  echo "1.0.0" > VERSION

#!/usr/bin/env bats
# test_workflow.bats — TDD tests for the semantic version bumper
#
# All functional tests run through the GitHub Actions workflow via `act`.
# Structural tests validate YAML and file references.
#
# Output from every act run is appended to act-result.txt.

# ── Setup / Teardown ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

ORIG_DIR=""
ACT_RESULT=""

setup_file() {
  ORIG_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$BATS_TEST_FILENAME")" && pwd)"
  ACT_RESULT="${ORIG_DIR}/act-result.txt"
  # Clear the results file at the start of the suite
  : > "$ACT_RESULT"
  export ORIG_DIR ACT_RESULT
}

setup() {
  ORIG_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$BATS_TEST_FILENAME")" && pwd)"
  ACT_RESULT="${ORIG_DIR}/act-result.txt"
}

# Helper: create a temporary git repo with all project files
create_test_repo() {
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir=$(mktemp -d)

  # Copy project files into the temp repo
  cp "${ORIG_DIR}/version-bumper.sh" "$tmpdir/"
  cp -r "${ORIG_DIR}/test" "$tmpdir/"
  mkdir -p "$tmpdir/.github/workflows"
  cp "${ORIG_DIR}/.github/workflows/semantic-version-bumper.yml" "$tmpdir/.github/workflows/"

  # Initialize git repo (act requires this)
  cd "$tmpdir"
  git init -b main
  git config user.email "test@test.com"
  git config user.name "Test"
  git add -A
  git commit -m "initial commit"

  echo "$tmpdir"
}

# Helper: run act and capture output, appending to act-result.txt
run_act() {
  local job="$1"
  shift
  local output
  local exit_code=0

  echo "===== ACT RUN: job=$job $(date -Iseconds) =====" >> "$ACT_RESULT"

  output=$(act push --rm \
    -j "$job" \
    -P ubuntu-latest=catthehacker/ubuntu:act-latest \
    --detect-event \
    "$@" 2>&1) || exit_code=$?

  echo "$output" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
  echo "===== EXIT CODE: $exit_code =====" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
  echo "" >> "$ACT_RESULT"

  # Make output available to the test
  echo "$output"
  return "$exit_code"
}

# ── Structural Tests ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

# RED: workflow YAML must exist
@test "workflow file exists" {
  [ -f "${ORIG_DIR}/.github/workflows/semantic-version-bumper.yml" ]
}

# RED: workflow must pass actionlint
@test "workflow passes actionlint" {
  run actionlint "${ORIG_DIR}/.github/workflows/semantic-version-bumper.yml"
  echo "actionlint output: $output" >> "$ACT_RESULT"
  [ "$status" -eq 0 ]
}

# RED: workflow must have correct triggers
@test "workflow has push and pull_request triggers" {
  local wf="${ORIG_DIR}/.github/workflows/semantic-version-bumper.yml"
  grep -q "push:" "$wf"
  grep -q "pull_request:" "$wf"
  grep -q "workflow_dispatch:" "$wf"
}

# RED: workflow must define expected jobs
@test "workflow has bump-version and test-cases jobs" {
  local wf="${ORIG_DIR}/.github/workflows/semantic-version-bumper.yml"
  grep -q "bump-version:" "$wf"
  grep -q "test-cases:" "$wf"
}

# RED: workflow references version-bumper.sh which must exist
@test "workflow references existing script files" {
  local wf="${ORIG_DIR}/.github/workflows/semantic-version-bumper.yml"
  grep -q "version-bumper.sh" "$wf"
  [ -f "${ORIG_DIR}/version-bumper.sh" ]
  [ -x "${ORIG_DIR}/version-bumper.sh" ]
}

# RED: workflow must use actions/checkout@v4
@test "workflow uses actions/checkout@v4" {
  local wf="${ORIG_DIR}/.github/workflows/semantic-version-bumper.yml"
  grep -q "actions/checkout@v4" "$wf"
}

# RED: workflow must set permissions
@test "workflow sets permissions" {
  local wf="${ORIG_DIR}/.github/workflows/semantic-version-bumper.yml"
  grep -q "permissions:" "$wf"
}

# RED: script passes shellcheck
@test "version-bumper.sh passes shellcheck" {
  run shellcheck "${ORIG_DIR}/version-bumper.sh"
  [ "$status" -eq 0 ]
}

# RED: script passes bash -n syntax check
@test "version-bumper.sh passes bash syntax check" {
  run bash -n "${ORIG_DIR}/version-bumper.sh"
  [ "$status" -eq 0 ]
}

# ── Functional Tests via act ────────────────────────────────────────────────

# RED: bump-version job must succeed with default fixtures
@test "act: bump-version job succeeds" {
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir=$(create_test_repo)
  cd "$tmpdir"

  # Create a VERSION file for the default bump-version job
  echo "1.0.0" > VERSION
  git add VERSION
  git commit -m "feat: add version file"

  local output
  output=$(run_act "bump-version")
  local rc=$?

  echo "$output"

  # Assert act exited cleanly
  [ "$rc" -eq 0 ]

  # Assert job succeeded
  echo "$output" | grep -q "Job succeeded"

  # Assert it produced version output — default uses minor fixture → 1.1.0
  echo "$output" | grep -q "VERSION_BUMPED=1.1.0"

  rm -rf "$tmpdir"
}

# RED: patch bump — fix commits on version 1.0.0 should produce 1.0.1
@test "act: patch bump 1.0.0 -> 1.0.1" {
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir=$(create_test_repo)
  cd "$tmpdir"
  git add -A && git commit --allow-empty -m "re-add" 2>/dev/null || true

  local output
  output=$(run_act "test-cases")
  local rc=$?

  echo "$output"
  [ "$rc" -eq 0 ]

  # Check the patch-bump matrix entry
  echo "$output" | grep -q "TEST=patch-bump"
  echo "$output" | grep -q "EXPECTED_VERSION=1.0.1"
  echo "$output" | grep "TEST=patch-bump" -A 5 | grep -q "PASS: Version matches (1.0.1)"

  rm -rf "$tmpdir"
}

# RED: minor bump — feat commits on version 1.1.0 should produce 1.2.0
@test "act: minor bump 1.1.0 -> 1.2.0" {
  local tmpdir
  tmpdir=$(create_test_repo)
  cd "$tmpdir"

  local output
  output=$(run_act "test-cases")
  local rc=$?

  echo "$output"
  [ "$rc" -eq 0 ]

  echo "$output" | grep -q "TEST=minor-bump"
